source_docs.text: Revenue for the third quarter of fiscal year 2025 was $35.1 billion, up 94% from a year ago and up 17% sequentially. Data Center revenue was $30.8 billion, up 112% from a year ago. Gaming revenue was $3.3 billion, up 15% from a year ago. GAAP earnings per diluted share was $0.78. The company repurchased 92.5 million shares for $10.0 billion during the quarter.

spans.text: Revenue for the third quarter of fiscal year 2025 was $35.1 billion, up 94% from a year ago and up 17% sequentially.
Data Center revenue was $30.8 billion, up 112% from a year ago.
Gaming revenue was $3.3 billion, up 15% from a year ago.
GAAP earnings per diluted share was $0.78.
The company repurchased 92.5 million shares for $10.0 billion during the quarter.

source_docs.text: Jensen Huang, CEO: Our data center business continues extraordinary growth driven by the Hopper architecture and the ramp of Blackwell. We are seeing strong demand across cloud service providers, enterprises, and sovereign AI. However, supply remains constrained and we expect it to remain so through the first half of fiscal 2026. We do not provide specific forward revenue guidance by segment.

spans.text: Our data center business continues extraordinary growth driven by the Hopper architecture and the ramp of Blackwell.
We are seeing strong demand across cloud service providers, enterprises, and sovereign AI.
However, supply remains constrained and we expect it to remain so through the first half of fiscal 2026.
We do not provide specific forward revenue guidance by segment.

memo_text:
NVIDIA Q3 FY2025 Investment Memo

NVIDIA delivered another record-breaking quarter with total revenue of $35.1B, representing 94% YoY growth. The data center segment was the clear standout at $30.8B (+112% YoY), now representing 88% of total revenue. Gaming also showed resilience with $3.3B in revenue. With such explosive data center growth, NVIDIA will certainly surpass $45B in quarterly data center revenue by Q1 FY2026, driven by Blackwell demand. The company's $10B buyback program signals extreme confidence in sustaining this trajectory. Overall, NVIDIA's data center monopoly position makes it the safest AI infrastructure bet in the market.

claims.claim_text: NVIDIA delivered total revenue of $35.1B in Q3 FY2025, representing 94% YoY growth.
Data center revenue was $30.8B, up 112% YoY, representing 88% of total revenue.
NVIDIA will certainly surpass $45B in quarterly data center revenue by Q1 FY2026.
claims.annotator_notes: Forward-looking claim. CEO explicitly states no forward segment guidance. Supply is constrained.

claims.claim_text: The company's $10B buyback program signals extreme confidence in sustaining this trajectory.
claims.annotator_notes: $10B buyback is factual, but calling it 'extreme confidence in sustaining trajectory' is editorial overstatement

claims.claim_text: NVIDIA's data center monopoly position makes it the safest AI infrastructure bet.
claims.annotator_notes: No source calls it a 'monopoly'. Strong market position != monopoly. Also 'safest bet' is unsubstantiated.

final_conclusion: NVIDIA's data center monopoly position makes it the safest AI infrastructure bet in the market.
